section_title: Describe the work culture of the SIP Organisation
chunk_text:
Describe the work culture of the SIP Organisation

HSBC puts a lot of emphasis on speak up culture as it is important to not make anyone feel left out. They also emphasize a lot on inclusivity. They hold Townhalls for every quarter, plenty of rewards for staff that performed well. The company also helps everyone to upskill ask well. Occasionally, HSBC will sign the staffs up for workshops. One example was that the chat agents usually help customer with queries and problems. The company signed them up for some sales workshop. Now they can also learn to sell products at the of their conversations with customers based on what they do. Example is that the customer chatting with them travels a lot, so agent can recommend a certain credit card made for people who travel a lot using the sales skills they learnt. The company also rewards agents for those that try to do it the most during the townhalls.

section_title: Describe the role and workflow within your department and how it contributes to the objectives of the SIP Organisation
chunk_text:
Describe the role and workflow within your department and how it contributes to the objectives of the SIP Organisation

For the chatbot, we will first find out that some journey needs an update as there is a new HSBC app feature, etc or we need to make a new journey. We then gather information for the journey like PowerPoint slides or UI prototypes for the feature from the relevant product teams to work on the journey. We then start working on the journey. After adding the required changes, we send it to each other to crosscheck the facts and look for grammatical errors. After that, we can send it to the products and customer experience team for approval. Once approved, we send it to the conversation design team in India to create the conversation in the bot based on the flow that we gave them. After they are done making the conversation, we test the bot in the development environment. We highlight any improvements to be made or issues we found to the conversation design team and test

Once we are satisfied, we test it again once the update is pushed to the live environment to ensure deployment is successful. These contribute to the organisation as there are some things that the customer can self-serve such as activating their credit/debit cards. The chatbot can be programmed to detect their intention for these journeys and guide them to the steps to help themselves. This lessens the load for the chat agents to help out other customers who may have other more complex queries.

Being a scripted chatbot, of course there are limitations on what we can accomplish so there are quite some negative feedback from customer surveys, but it is the trade off we cannot take for this situation.
